questions.texte: Explain what 'reported speech' means.
questions.reponse: Reported speech is when we report what someone said without using their exact words.

questions.texte: Identify the reporting verbs commonly used in reported speech.
questions.reponse: said, told, explained, announced, stated

questions.texte: Explain what 'tense backshift' means.
questions.reponse: Tense backshift is when we change the tense of the verb in reported speech: present becomes past, past becomes past perfect.

questions.texte: Give examples of time and place adverbs that change in reported speech.
questions.reponse: now → then, today → that day, yesterday → the day before, here → there, this → that

enonce.texte: Change the following direct statements into reported speech. Pay attention to tense backshift.

questions.texte: Direct: 'I am happy,' she said.
questions.reponse: She said that she was happy.

questions.texte: Direct: 'We are going to the market,' they said.
questions.reponse: They said that they were going to the market.

questions.texte: Direct: 'He works hard,' she told me.
questions.reponse: She told me that he worked hard.

questions.texte: Direct: 'I have finished my homework,' he said.
questions.reponse: He said that he had finished his homework.

questions.texte: Direct: 'They will come tomorrow,' she announced.
questions.reponse: She announced that they would come the next day.

enonce.texte: Report the following statements, making sure to change pronouns and time/place adverbs correctly.

questions.texte: Direct: 'I will see you here tomorrow,' she said to me.
questions.reponse: She told me that she would see me there the next day.

questions.texte: Direct: 'We are leaving now,' they said.
questions.reponse: They said that they were leaving then.

questions.texte: Direct: 'This is my book,' he said.
questions.reponse: He said that that was his book.

questions.texte: Direct: 'We met here yesterday,' they explained.
questions.reponse: They explained that they had met there the day before.

enonce.texte: Complete the sentences with the correct reporting verb: said, told, explained, announced, stated.

questions.texte: She ________ that she was going to visit her grandmother.
questions.reponse: said

questions.texte: He ________ me that he had finished his work.
questions.reponse: told

questions.texte: The teacher ________ that the exam would be next week.
questions.reponse: announced

questions.texte: The manager ________ that the company was doing well.
questions.reponse: stated

questions.texte: She ________ to me that she was tired.
questions.reponse: said

enonce.texte: Find and correct the errors in the following reported speech sentences.

questions.texte: She said that she is tired.
questions.reponse: She said that she was tired.

questions.texte: He told that he was leaving.
questions.reponse: He said that he was leaving. OR He told me that he was leaving.

questions.texte: They said that they will arrive tomorrow.
questions.reponse: They said that they would arrive the next day.

questions.texte: She told me that she has finished her homework.
questions.reponse: She told me that she had finished her homework.

questions.texte: He said that he come to school yesterday.
questions.reponse: He said that he had come to school the day before.

enonce.texte:
Read the dialogue below and report it in indirect speech.

Dialogue between Teacher and Student:

Teacher: "You must finish your homework by Friday."

Student: "I have already finished it, Sir."

Teacher: "I am very proud of you."

Student: "Thank you, Sir. I worked hard on it."

questions.texte: Transform the dialogue into reported speech.
questions.reponse: The teacher told the student that he/she must finish the homework by Friday. The student said that he/she had already finished it. The teacher said that he/she was very proud of the student. The student thanked the teacher and said that he/she had worked hard on it.

intitule: Writing Practice: Reporting a News Article

enonce.texte:
Read the news article below and write a report summarizing what the people said using reported speech.

Local News Interview:

Mayor: "We will build a new hospital in the city center."

Doctor: "This is very good news for the community."

Citizen: "I hope the construction will finish quickly."

Nurse: "We need more equipment in the current hospital."

questions.texte: Summarize the interview using reported speech.
questions.reponse: The mayor announced that they would build a new hospital in the city center. The doctor said that that was very good news for the community. A citizen said that he/she hoped the construction would finish quickly. A nurse stated that they needed more equipment in the current hospital.

intitule: Real-Life Situation: Reporting a Phone Call

enonce.texte:
Imagine you received a phone call from your friend. Report what your friend said to your parents.

Your friend said:

  • "I am coming to your house tomorrow."
  • "I have bought some snacks."
  • "We can watch a movie together."
  • "I will call you when I leave."

questions.texte: Report your friend's statements to your parents.
questions.reponse: My friend said that he/she was coming to our house the next day. He/she said that he/she had bought some snacks. He/she said that we could watch a movie together. He/she said that he/she would call me when he/she left.

enonce.texte:
Read the following direct speech passage and transform it into reported speech.

Student Presentation:

"Good morning everyone," said John. "I have prepared a presentation on climate change. I have been researching this topic for two months. I will show you some important statistics. We must all work together to protect our environment. I hope you will find this presentation interesting."

questions.texte: Transform John's speech into reported speech.
questions.reponse: John greeted everyone and said that he had prepared a presentation on climate change. He said that he had been researching that topic for two months. He said that he would show them some important statistics. He said that they all must work together to protect their environment. He said that he hoped they would find that presentation interesting.
